sentences.base_language: I woke up late this morning.
sentences.grammarNote: اِستَيقَظتُ (istayqaẓtu, I woke up) — Form X of root ي-ق-ظ (y-q-ẓ, to be awake): اِستَيقَظَ = to wake up (inchoative/reflexive Form X). The past tense suffix تُ (-tu) = I. مُتَأَخِّراً (mutaʾakhkhiran, late) — Form V active participle of أ-خ-ر (ʾ-kh-r, to delay): تَأَخَّرَ = to be late, مُتَأَخِّر = one who is late. Used here as an accusative adverb.

sentences.base_language: I ran to the bus stop but the bus had already left.
sentences.target_language: ركضت إلى محطة الحافلة لكن الحافلة كانت قد غادرت بالفعل.
sentences.romanized: rakadtu ila mahattat al-hafila lakin al-hafila kanat qad ghadarat bil-fi'l.
sentences.literal: I-ran to stop the-bus but the-bus was-it already departed in-the-fact.
sentences.grammarNote: كانَت قَد + past verb = past perfect (had already done). قَد (qad) before a past verb emphasizes completion: كانت قد غادرت = she/it had already left. This is how Arabic expresses 'had done' (the pluperfect).

كُنتُ مُحبَطاً (kuntu muḥbaṭan, I was frustrated) — كَان (to be, past) + adjective in the accusative: the predicate of كَان takes the accusative case. مُحبَط (muḥbaṭ, frustrated) — passive participle Form IV of ح-ب-ط (ḥ-b-ṭ, to frustrate/cancel): أَحبَطَ = to frustrate, مُحبَط = frustrated. جِدَّاً (jiddan, very) intensifies the adjective.

sentences.base_language: I decided to walk to work.
sentences.grammarNote: قَرَّرتُ (qarrart, I decided) — Form II of ق-ر-ر (q-r-r, to settle/decide): قَرَّرَ = to decide (intensive doubling of the root). قَرَّرَ + أَن + verb = decided to do: قَرَّرتُ أَن أَمشِي = I decided to walk. أَمشِي (amshī, I walk) — Form I of م-ش-ي (m-sh-y, to walk): a weak verb ending in ي.

sentences.base_language: On the way I spilled coffee on my shirt.
sentences.target_language: في الطريق سكبت القهوة على قميصي.
sentences.literal: In the-way I-spilled the-coffee on my-shirt.
sentences.grammarNote: سَكَبتُ (sakabtu, I spilled) — Form I of س-ك-ب (s-k-b, to pour/spill): a simple action verb. الطَّرِيق (aṭ-ṭarīq, the way/road) — from root ط-ر-ق (ṭ-r-q, to knock/beat a path). قَمِيصِي (qamīṣī, my shirt) — قَمِيص (shirt, from root ق-م-ص) + suffix ي (my). عَلى (ʿalā, on/over) is the preposition for surfaces and contact.

sentences.base_language: I said to myself: what a terrible morning!
sentences.target_language: قلت لنفسي: يا لها من صباح فظيع!
sentences.romanized: qultu li-nafsi: ya laha min sabah fazhi'!
sentences.literal: I-said to-myself: O for-it from morning terrible!
sentences.grammarNote: يا لَها مِن (ya laha min) is an Arabic exclamation meaning 'what a…!'. The pronoun ها (ha) agrees with the feminine noun صباح? No — صباح is masculine, but يا له من is fixed. For feminine nouns: يا لها من فكرة رائعة! (what a wonderful idea!). It is a fixed exclamatory formula.

sentences.base_language: At work I also forgot my phone at home.
sentences.target_language: في العمل نسيت هاتفي في البيت أيضاً.
sentences.romanized: fi al-'amal nasitu hatifi fi al-bayt aydan.
sentences.literal: At the-work I-forgot my-phone in the-house also.
sentences.grammarNote: نَسِيتُ (nasītu, I forgot) — Form I of ن-س-ي (n-s-y, to forget): a weak verb ending in ي. The past-tense suffix تُ = I. هَاتِفِي (hātifī, my phone) — هَاتِف (phone, active participle فَاعِل of ه-ت-ف, to call out) + suffix ي (my). الهَاتِف literally means 'the caller/calier' — originally a voice heard without a visible speaker.

sentences.target_language: لم أستطع التركيز على أي شيء.
sentences.literal: Not I-was-able the-focusing on any thing.
sentences.grammarNote: لَم أَستَطِع (lam astatiʿ, I could not) — لَم + jussive verb (present tense with dropped ending). لَم negates the past using the present jussive: لَم يَذهَب (he did not go), لَم أَستَطِع (I could not). اِستَطَاعَ (istaṭāʿa, to be able) — Form X of ط-و-ع (ṭ-w-ʿ, to obey/be pliable): capability. التَّركِيز (at-tarkīz, focusing) — Form II verbal noun of ر-ك-ز (r-k-z, to concentrate).

sentences.base_language: My colleague saw that I was upset and brought me tea.
sentences.target_language: زميلي رأى أنني كنت منزعجاً وأحضر لي شاياً.
sentences.romanized: zamili ra'a annani kuntu munza'ijan wa-ahdara li sha'yan.
sentences.literal: My-colleague saw that-I was upset and-brought for-me tea.
sentences.grammarNote: رَأَى (raʾā, saw) — Form I of ر-أ-ي (r-ʾ-y, to see): a defective verb. Past tense third person singular. أَنَّنِي كُنتُ (annanī kuntu, that I was) — أَنَّ (that, emphatic conjunction) + suffix نِي (I) + past verb. مُنزَعِجاً (munzaʿijan, upset — accusative) — Form VII active participle of ن-ز-ع (n-z-ʿ, to be annoyed): اِنزَعَجَ = to be disturbed/upset.

sentences.base_language: That small kindness made me feel a little better.
sentences.target_language: هذا اللطف الصغير جعلني أشعر بتحسن بسيط.
sentences.romanized: hadha al-lutf as-saghir ja'alani ash'ur bi-tahassun basit.
sentences.literal: This the-kindness the-small made-me I-feel with-improvement slight.
sentences.grammarNote: جَعَلَنِي أَشعُر (jaʿalanī ashʿur, made me feel) — جَعَلَ (to make/cause, causative verb) + suffix نِي (me) + present verb without أَن: this pattern 'made me do X' uses جَعَل + pronoun + bare verb. اللُّطف الصَّغِير (al-luṭf aṣ-ṣaghīr) — definite noun + definite adjective. لُطف (kindness) from root ل-ط-ف (l-ṭ-f, to be gentle): لَطِيف (kind/gentle).

sentences.base_language: Tonight I will relax and try to sleep early.
sentences.grammarNote: سَأَسترخِي (sa-astarkhi, I will relax) — سَـ (near future) + Form X of ر-خ-و: اِسترخَى = to relax. أُحَاوِل (uḥāwil, I try) — Form III of ح-و-ل (ḥ-w-l, to surround/try): حَاوَلَ = to attempt/try. النَّوم (an-nawm, sleep/sleeping) — verbal noun Form I of ن-و-م (n-w-m): نَامَ (to sleep), نَوم (sleep), نَائِم (sleeping). مُبَكِّراً (mubakkiran, early) — accusative adverb.

sentences.base_language: Tomorrow will be a better day.
sentences.grammarNote: أَفْضَل (afdal) = better / best. Arabic comparatives are formed on the أَفْعَل (af'al) pattern: كبير (big) → أكبر (bigger), جيد (good) → أفضل or أجود (better). No separate word for 'more' is needed.
